title: Buchenavia tetraphylla
intro: Buchenavia tetraphylla, also known as Quine or "Tree of Life", is a medicinal plant native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the family Combretaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Buchenavia tetraphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Buchenavia tetraphylla is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to treat various ailments, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements and capsules.
taxonomy: Buchenavia tetraphylla is classified within the family Combretaceae. This plant is native to the Americas and belongs to the genus Buchenavia. The Combretaceae family is a group of flowering plants, commonly known as the ruddlewood or silk floss trees family. Buchenavia tetraphylla is a species of tree that is native to Central and South America. Within the Combretaceae family, Buchenavia tetraphylla is closely related to other species of trees in the same genus.
